It engaged in business on a nationwide scale by putting advertisements and other types of promotional materials in newspapers, and the reason it put those materials in newspapers is that the existing 1876 law that this Court upheld in the Jackson case did not apply at that time for any of the other states In the Nation or Congress — excuse me — or the executive to do anything about the existence of the Louisiana Lottery. The result of that was the Anti-Lottery Act of 1890. Congress passed that law in order to deny the Louisiana Lottery the opportunity to use newspapers for -- for promoting their own financial success.
